hero.tagline: A simple line for transparent governance and common sense.

about.heading: About
about.paragraph1: I began public service with questions learned by listening in the field. The experience I've gained over the years has shown me one thing clearly: good governance is born not from grand promises, but from sustainable, accountable small steps.
about.paragraph2: My purpose here today is to share every decision I make with you, to keep my work traceable, and to always keep my door open.
about.quote: “Politics is measured not by what is said, but by what is done.”

items.title: Open Budget Initiative
items.description: A project making public spending visible to everyone through plain-language, regular reports.

items.title: Neighborhood Councils
items.description: A network of regular meetings where decisions are made in the neighborhood, not behind a desk, and residents have a direct voice.

items.title: Youth Policy Workshop
items.description: An education and mentorship program aiming to make young people founding partners in politics, not just spectators.

posts.title: Why Local Transparency Can Also Work at the National Scale
posts.description: A short assessment of how opening up budget data helps rebuild trust.

posts.title: Listening Is a Form of Governance
posts.description: Notes and lessons from the first six months of the neighborhood councils.

posts.title: Politics Built With Young People
posts.description: Observations from the first term of the Youth Policy Workshop.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.intro: You can reach me for feedback, suggestions, and collaboration requests.
